title: Aider Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and techniques for using Aider effectively in coding tasks.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the model's output to low for deterministic results.
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output.

commands.description: Uses the GPT-4o model with streaming responses for real-time interaction.
commands.scenario: When you need quick feedback during a live coding session.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the model to reason through problems methodically.
commands.scenario: When solving complex algorithmic problems that require logical reasoning.

commands.description: Limits the response length to manage cost and focus on concise answers.
commands.scenario: When you need to control API usage costs while getting detailed responses.

commands.description: Increases creativity by allowing more randomness in responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative code snippets or brainstorming ideas.

commands.command: Use specific examples to clarify your request.
commands.description: Helps the model understand context better by providing concrete examples.
commands.scenario: When the model needs more context to generate accurate code.

commands.description: Uses nucleus sampling to balance creativity and coherence in responses.
commands.scenario: When you need a mix of creative and coherent code suggestions.

commands.description: Prevents the model from generating specific tokens, like end-of-text.
commands.scenario: When you want to avoid certain outputs in generated code.

commands.command: Provide a high-level overview before diving into details.
commands.description: Improves comprehension by setting context before detailed code generation.
commands.scenario: When the task requires understanding complex systems or architectures.

commands.description: Reduces repetition in generated text by penalizing frequent tokens.
commands.scenario: When generating documentation or comments that should be varied.

commands.description: Encourages the model to introduce new topics by penalizing existing ones.
commands.scenario: When exploring new coding patterns or technologies.

commands.command: Summarize the problem before solving it.
commands.description: Ensures the model fully understands the problem before generating a solution.
commands.scenario: When tackling complex coding challenges that require clarity.

commands.description: Specifies stop sequences to control where the model should stop generating text.
commands.scenario: When you need to ensure code snippets end at logical points.

commands.description: Switch to architect mode to separate high-level design reasoning from code implementation, reducing logic errors in complex refactors.
Adjust the number of tokens used for the repository map to balance context window usage against the depth of codebase awareness.
Examine the proposed changes in a unified diff format before applying them to ensure precision in code modification.

commands.command: Use the /add <file> command to selectively include files in the chat context to prevent context window saturation.
commands.description: Manually manage the context window by explicitly adding only the necessary files for the current task.

commands.command: Refactor this code by applying the SOLID principles and ensuring 100% test coverage for the new logic.
commands.description: A prompt-engineering pattern that forces the LLM to adhere to specific architectural standards and verification requirements.
